name: Kinetic
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a geometric sans-serif; geometry is present in the simple character structure but optical corrections soften any strict mechanical rigidity, placing it within the humanised geometric tradition.
summary: A geometric sans-serif in three weights with italics, designed by Noel Pretorius and María Ramos and inspired by the playful wire sculptures of Alexander Calder.
description: Released in 2017, Kinetic draws on the light, soft visual quality of Calder's kinetic mobiles, translating that sensibility into type forms that avoid rigid mechanical geometry. Optical corrections and character-by-character interpretation keep the design warm and readable across print and screen. The family suits editorial, advertising and corporate or art direction work where a contemporary geometric sans is needed without the coldness of strict construction.
foundry_blurb: NM Type describe Kinetic as a geometric sans-serif that avoids strict design patterns, with geometry present in the simple character structures but tempered by optical corrections and room for individual character interpretation. Inspired primarily by Alexander Calder's kinetic sculptures, the family comes in three weights with italics and is intended as a functional text typeface suited to advertising, editorial and corporate or art projects across both print and screen.

name: Sastre
classification_notes: Sastre mixes the mechanical structure of a wedge serif with old-style design features, including moderate contrast and angled axis, making it a hybrid slab-influenced text serif with historical and unconventional influences.
summary: A hybrid wedge serif by María Ramos that blends mechanical structure with old-style features, designed for long-form text and small sizes across five weights and a variable font.
description: Conceived in 2014 during María Ramos's studies at the University of Reading, Sastre draws on the mechanical look of a wedge serif while incorporating old-style design traits such as moderate contrast, an angled axis and wide characters. The family runs from Regular to Extrabold with a variable font, and includes monospaced small caps suited to vertical type settings alongside a collection of stitch ornaments for borders and patterns. The italic is still in development; the upright cuts and variable font are available exclusively from NM Type.
foundry_blurb: NM Type describe Sastre as a hybrid typeface where nature and craftsmanship meet, with a broken structure and generous proportions that perform well in long-form text and at small sizes. Rather than echoing traditional forms, it reinterprets them and challenges conventional ideals of beauty and refinement in contemporary text typography, mixing the mechanical look of a wedge serif with old-style design features.

name: Sixten
classification_notes: Though it has Latin letterforms, Sixten's defining character comes from its deliberately unconventional, child-inspired skeleton and runic undertones, placing it firmly in the display/expressive category rather than conventional sans-serif.
summary: A variable Latin typeface drawn from a child's pre-instructed letterforms, yielding shapes with runic and punk overtones across three width axes.
description: Designed by Noel Pretorius and María Ramos at NM Type, Sixten takes its cues from the pre-taught handwriting of a four-year-old, tracing unexpected connections between instinctive mark-making and early Phoenician and Greek letter shapes. The family spans fifteen static fonts across Narrow, Standard and Wide widths, each in five weights from Regular to Black, plus a variable font that shifts both width and weight axes simultaneously. Twelve stylistic sets enable dynamic combinations including mirror writing, flying small caps and flag caps, giving designers extensive scope to work against typographic convention.
foundry_blurb: NM Type describe Sixten as a playful type family inspired by the unbridled creativity of a child, named after a founder's son whose intuitive four-year-old letterforms sparked the project. The design process revealed links between his elementary shapes and the Phoenician and Early Greek alphabet, prompting broader questions about how writing rules limit natural instinct. The variable font shifts from Narrow to Wide, affecting not just width but the skeleton of each letter, while twelve stylistic sets offer expressive alternates including mirror writing and flying small caps.

name: Movement
classification_notes: A display typeface with four design extremes across two axes (weight and directness of stroke), inspired by dance movement theory; the expressive, concept-driven letterforms place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A free variable font by Noel Pretorius and María Ramos, built from the tracked movement paths of South African dancer Andile Vellem at Design Indaba 2019.
description: Commissioned in collaboration with Design Indaba, the typeface translates Rudolf Laban's movement theory into type: direct movement is rendered as bound, straight strokes; indirect movement as flexible, curved shapes. The variable font spans four design extremes — Direct Thin, Direct Black, Indirect Thin and Indirect Black — with continuous variation between them representing changes of movement through time. Available as a free download.
foundry_blurb: NM Type describe Movement as a variable font inspired by dance movements, begun as a collaboration with Design Indaba and South African contemporary dancer Andile Vellem. By tracking Andile's performance and using the resulting paths as a reference, Noel Pretorius and María Ramos shaped the letterforms around Laban's three movement characteristics: weight, space and time.
